page: Microsoft Azure E64-16ds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the EDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores operating at 2.8 GHz. The server is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core. It includes 2458 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top 25%. Read-only memory bandwidth reaches 834.17 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 103.1 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, while write bandwidth is measured at 59.48 GB/sec. CPU-bound benchmarks, such as stress-ng single-core (1899.48) and multi-core (15535.97) tests, fall into the average category. Cryptographic and vector operations are accelerated by hardware flags including AVX-512 and AES.

The primary architectural tradeoff of this instance is its constrained CPU-to-memory ratio, which provides a massive 512 GB memory footprint while limiting compute resources to 16 vCPUs. This configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not scale linearly with CPU cores, minimizing software licensing costs tied to vCPU counts. It is optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and memory-heavy data processing pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure E64-16ds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the EDSv5 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs, 512.0 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, with support for Premium Storage. The hardware configuration delivers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 32.0 GB per core, making it highly efficient for memory-bound workloads. Benchmark data shows strong memory performance, with a Passmark memory score in the top 25% and cached read bandwidth of 834.17 GB/sec. Conversely, CPU performance remains in the average tier, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 15535.97. This resource distribution makes the server a cost-effective option for database operations, Redis caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that require massive memory capacity without the licensing overhead of a high vCPU count.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16ds_v5 is a memory-optimized server featuring 16 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage. It offers strong memory performance for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64-16ds_v5 is a memory-optimized server with 16 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage, built for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server with a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 16 dedicated vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM
High memory density of 32 GB per core with strong Passmark memory performance
Bundled 2458 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Hardware-accelerated cryptography and vector operations via AVX-512 and AES
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications
